title: Tech Disruptors: Booking.com on Global Demand, AI in Travel
summary: “One of the things that’s been universally true ... is the human desire to travel is really strong. It’s very consistent,” says James Waters, chief business officer of Booking.com. Waters joins Bloomberg Intelligence analyst Nicole D’Souza on this episode of the Tech Disruptors podcast to discuss the outlook for global travel demand. title: Andy Burnham Says He’s Pro-Business in his First Speech as Labour Leader
summary: 40 years of pain haven’t stopped Burnham dreaming. The new Labour leader is certain he has a plan for all.

title: Chip Stocks Fall to Bear-Market Brink in AI Unwind: Markets Wrap
summary: A selloff in chipmakers gathered pace, sending the group that has led this year’s stock rally to the edge of a bear market on worries that the artificial-intelligence spending spree is becoming harder to justify.

title: US Oil Rig Count Notches Longest Streak Without Drop Since 2022
summary: Drilling in the US oil patch achieved the longest stretch without a decrease in activity in more than four years, supporting shale’s expansion amid higher prices driven by the Iran war.

title: US Stocks Decline as Traders Fear Another ‘DeepSeek Moment’
summary: US stocks fell on Friday as semiconductor names dipped following the reveal of an artificial-intelligence model from a Chinese startup.

title: Griffin Wants to Buy Old Cottage Across From New Citadel HQ
summary: Ken Griffin has gone to extraordinary lengths to assemble parcels of land around his planned Citadel headquarters in Miami, including buying up all 138 units in a condo building across the street.

title: Blue Owl-Backed Latigo Files for IPO, Adding to Biotech Pipeline
summary: Latigo Biotherapeutics Inc. filed for an initial public offering, joining a growing list of biotechnology firms considering going public as soon as this quarter.

title: ‘Sea of Red’ Hits Emerging Stocks as AI Concerns Jolts Markets
summary: Emerging-market stocks slumped, with a key gauge headed for a technical correction on mounting concerns the outlook for artificial intelligence no longer justifies the industry’s elevated valuations.

title: San Antonio to Borrow $944 Million for Major Airport Expansion
summary: San Antonio, one of the fastest-growing US metro areas, is tapping the bond market for almost $1 billion as part of an ambitious program to expand and improve its airport.

title: Why the Moonshot Moment May Not Be a DeepSeek Moment
summary: History doesn’t always repeat itself.

title: Amundi Says Oil, Gas Crucial to EU’s Clean-Energy Transition
summary: Amundi SA wants the European Union to free asset managers to add oil and gas exposures to a new fund category intended to support the transition to a lower-carbon economy.

title: Gold Trims Weekly Drop on Dip-Buying Despite Rate Hike Odds
summary: Gold trimmed its weekly loss as some investors bought on dips after the precious metal broke below a key price level, even as escalating war tensions kept interest rate hike bets alive.

title: Telecom Italia Is Said to Consider €10.8 Billion Poste Bid Fair
summary: Telecom Italia SpA’s board is leaning toward endorsing Poste Italiane SpA’s €10.8 billion ($12.4 billion) takeover offer as fair, and is unlikely to seek a higher price, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 17
summary: Oil surged on growing fears the US and Iran could return to full war, threatening flows through the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Foundever Strikes Deal With Creditors to Cut Debt Load
summary: Troubled business services giant Foundever Group has reached an agreement with a group of creditors to reduce its multi-billion dollar debt load, with the company’s controlling shareholder injecting fresh funds and installing new management.

title: Anglo Selects Penny’s Group as Preferred Bidder for De Beers
summary: Anglo American Plc has selected a group led by De Beers’ former boss Gareth Penny as the preferred bidder for its sale of the world’s biggest diamond mining company, people with knowledge of the matter said.

title: Sweetgreen Pares 26% Drop as Parasite Tied to Taco Bell Lettuce
summary: Sweetgreen Inc. shares surged on Friday, halting a four-day skid, after US regulators said a parasite outbreak that’s sickened thousands across several states was traced to shredded iceberg lettuce served at Taco Bell restaurants.

title: European Stocks Drop on Mideast Escalation Fears, Tech Selloff
summary: European shares extended declines Friday as geopolitical tensions and a technology-stock selloff weighed on markets.

title: US Gasoline Prices Are Heading to $4 a Gallon Again
summary: It’s the middle of summer driving season, when Americans typically embark on road trips and burn through millions of barrels of gasoline each day. They’ll be paying more for every fill up.

title: Crop Markets Mixed on Shipping Worries, US-China Trade Concerns
summary: Crop markets were mixed Friday amid lingering tensions in the Black Sea and Strait of Hormuz, as well as concerns over US trade ties with China.

title: CoreLogic Tweaks Terms on Struggling Debt Sale, Boosts Loan
summary: CoreLogic Inc. amended the terms of its roughly $5.3 billion debt offering and shifted part of the funding to loans from bonds after struggling with lukewarm investor demand.

title: How New PM Andy Burnham Affects Your Money and UK Markets: Live Q&A
summary: Bloomberg reporters answer your questions about the UK’s new prime minister Andy Burnham and what his policies mean for your money and markets in a Live Q&A on Tuesday, July 16 at 2 p.m. BST.
